title: cydonia vulgaris pyriformis
intro: Cydonia vulgaris pyriformis, commonly known as Quince, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Southern Europe. The medicinal properties of Quince lie in its fruit, leaves, and bark, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Quince include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce menstrual cramps, and lower cholesterol levels, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Quince is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in jams, preserves, and culinary dishes to harness its medicinal properties and flavor.
